target_cmd: curl -s https://www.solaceagi.com/agents -w '\n%{time_total}' -o /tmp/agents-response.html && echo "H1_FOUND=$(grep -c 'Your AI Agent' /tmp/agents-response.html)" && echo "DOWNLOAD_FOUND=$(grep -c 'Download' /tmp/agents-response.html)" && echo "MCP_FOUND=$(grep -c 'MCP' /tmp/agents-response.html)"
target_name: SolaceAGI Agents Page — Hook Test (MrBeast Oracle)
context: MrBeast oracle (q-agi-025): Does the /agents page have a compelling H1, download CTAs, and MCP mention? Check: 'Your AI Agent' H1 present, Download buttons present (count > 0), MCP integration mentioned. Load time from time_total — should be < 1s warm. The first 500 bytes of server-rendered HTML are always in <head>; this test correctly checks the full rendered page for value proposition presence. MrBeast: 'If the hook isn't in the page, you've lost.' Previous spec used head -c 500 which only checked <head> section — corrected to grep full file.
